defects.title: MFA backup codes generated in incomplete format are rejected during login recovery
defects.description: Generated and downloaded MFA backup codes are displayed as XXXX-XXXX-, while the MFA challenge expects XXXX-XXXX-XX. Manual entry and automation both remain on the MFA challenge and show invalid backup-code behavior.
defects.errorMessage: Backup codes generated/downloaded in format XXXX-XXXX- are rejected by the MFA challenge that expects XXXX-XXXX-XX.
defects.businessImpact: Users may be unable to recover access with backup codes if they lose authenticator access.
defects.recommendedInvestigationAction: Review backup-code generation, download/export formatting, storage validation, and MFA challenge validation rules so generated codes match the accepted login format.

defects.title: Google-authenticated users are asked for password when regenerating backup codes or disabling 2FA
defects.description: Google OAuth users do not have an application password, but MFA backup-code regeneration and disable-2FA flows require password confirmation.
defects.errorMessage: Google-authenticated user cannot regenerate backup codes or disable 2FA because the flow asks for a password the user does not have.
defects.businessImpact: Google-authenticated users may be unable to manage or recover MFA settings without admin/support intervention.
defects.recommendedInvestigationAction: Update MFA management confirmation for OAuth users to use an OAuth-safe verification method, such as recent login, TOTP confirmation, email verification, or step-up authentication.
